models.name: vasicek
models.label: Vasicek
models.short_description: Gaussian mean reversion with closed-form bond pricing.
models.notes: Fast, interpretable, and analytically convenient, but it allows negative rates.

models.name: dothan
models.label: Dothan
models.notes: Preserves positivity, but is less tractable and tends to fit curves less cleanly.

models.name: exp_vasicek
models.label: Exponentiated Vasicek
models.short_description: Mean reversion in log-rates with positive levels.
models.notes: Keeps rates positive and mean reverting, but bond pricing is not affine and is handled numerically here.

models.name: cir
models.label: CIR
models.notes: Strong default for positive-rate regimes: mean reverting, analytic, and often stable.

models.name: ho_lee
models.label: Ho-Lee
models.short_description: Exact initial-curve fit with Gaussian dynamics.
models.notes: Fits the starting curve exactly, but does not mean revert.

models.name: hull_white
models.label: Hull-White
models.short_description: Mean-reverting Gaussian model with exact initial-curve fit.
models.notes: A very practical blend of curve-fit flexibility and analytic tractability.

models.name: cir_pp
models.label: CIR++
models.short_description: Deterministic-shift extension of CIR for exact curve fitting.
models.notes: Keeps much of CIR's structure while matching the initial term structure more closely.

models.name: hjm
models.label: Gaussian HJM
models.short_description: Forward-curve model with flexible curve dynamics.
models.notes: Flexible and curve-aware, but less parsimonious than classic short-rate models.
